name: Coconut-Pineapple Bars
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine 1/2 cup softened margarine and 3/4 cup sugar in a small bowl.
Beat with an electric mixer set on medium speed until the mixture is light and fluffy.
Gradually beat in flour to form a soft dough.
2. Press the dough evenly onto the bottom and up the sides of a 9 x 9 x 1-3/4 inch
ungreased pan. Bake in a preheated 350-degree oven for 15 minutes or until golden brown.
Let cool.
3. Drain the pineapple very thoroughly. Spread the drained pineapple over the crust.
4. Add the remaining sugar to the egg; beat until just blended.
Add 1 tablespoon melted margarine, vanilla extract, and dried coconut.
Spread the mixture evenly over the pineapple.
5. Bake for an additional 20 minutes or until the top is golden brown.
6. Cool, then cut into 1-1/2 inch squares.
Yield: about 3 dozen.
